requirement: Add tenant selector to admin dashboard, inject X-GarudaX-Tenant header into all API calls, show tenant context in pages

tasks.title: TenantContext provider + apiFetch integration
tasks.description:
Create a TenantContext React context that holds the current tenant selection and injects the X-GarudaX-Tenant header into all API calls.

Part A — Create src/admin-ui/src/contexts/TenantContext.tsx:
- TenantInfo type: { id: string, name: string, status: string, flagship: boolean }
- TenantContextType: { currentTenant: TenantInfo | null, tenants: TenantInfo[], setCurrentTenant: (id: string) => void, isLoading: boolean }
- TenantProvider component: fetches tenants from /platform/v1/tenants on mount, stores in state, defaults to first ACTIVE tenant, persists selection to localStorage('garudax-tenant')
- useTenant() hook: returns TenantContextType

Part B — Update src/admin-ui/src/services/api.ts:
- Modify the apiFetch helper to read the current tenant from a module-level variable
- Add setApiTenant(tenantId: string) function that sets the tenant for all subsequent API calls
- In apiFetch: if tenant is set, add X-GarudaX-Tenant header to every request
- The TenantProvider calls setApiTenant when tenant changes
- Platform API calls (/platform/v1/*) should NOT include the tenant header

Part C — Wire TenantProvider into src/admin-ui/src/pages/DashboardLayout.tsx:
- Wrap the existing providers with <TenantProvider>
- Place it OUTSIDE KPIProvider so all child components can access tenant context

CX ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
□ Loading state: TenantProvider shows nothing extra while loading tenants
□ Empty state: if no tenants, show warning but don't block UI
□ Error state: if tenant fetch fails, fall back to no-tenant mode (platform pages still work)
□ No raw technical errors visible to user
□ Consistent styling: N/A (context provider, no UI)

Run: cd src/admin-ui && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test -- --run

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T1.md

tasks.title: Tenant selector in TopBar + dashboard indicator
tasks.description:
Add a tenant selector dropdown to the TopBar and a tenant indicator card to DashboardHome.

Part A — TopBar tenant selector (src/admin-ui/src/components/TopBar.tsx + TopBar.module.css):
- Import useTenant from TenantContext
- Add a select dropdown (or styled button+menu) showing current tenant name + StatusBadge
- When user selects a different tenant, call setCurrentTenant(id)
- Show tenant list as dropdown options: each with name + status badge (ACTIVE green, ONBOARDING yellow)
- Flagship tenants get a star icon or '(flagship)' suffix
- Position: left side of TopBar, after any existing elements
- Style: compact dropdown, max-width 200px, matches existing TopBar styling

Part B — DashboardHome tenant indicator (src/admin-ui/src/pages/DashboardHome.tsx + .module.css):
- Add a prominent banner/card at the top of the dashboard showing:
  - Current tenant name (large text)
  - Tenant status badge
  - Flagship indicator if applicable
  - Tenant ID in muted text below
- If no tenant selected, show 'No tenant selected — choose from the dropdown above'

CX ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all must pass):
□ Loading state: shows skeleton while tenants load in TopBar
□ Empty state: 'No tenants' message if list is empty
□ Error state: if fetch fails, TopBar shows 'No tenant' gracefully
□ Responsive: dropdown works on mobile (375px) — full-width on mobile
□ Keyboard navigation: select/dropdown reachable via Tab, options via Arrow keys
□ Accessible: <select> has <label> (visually hidden), aria-label on tenant indicator
□ Toast/feedback: toast when tenant is switched ('Switched to MSE Equities')
□ Form validation: N/A
□ No raw technical errors visible to user
□ Consistent styling: uses project CSS modules and StatusBadge component

Run: cd src/admin-ui && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test -- --run

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T2.md

tasks.title: Update securities pages to show tenant context
tasks.description:
Update the 3 securities pages to show which tenant's data they're displaying.

Read src/admin-ui/src/pages/SecuritiesInstruments.tsx, SecuritiesOrders.tsx, SecuritiesPositions.tsx.
Import useTenant from TenantContext.

For each page:
1. Get currentTenant from useTenant()
2. Update the page title to include tenant name: e.g., 'Securities Instruments — MSE Equities' or 'Securities Instruments — ACE Commodities'
3. If no tenant selected, show a message: 'Select a tenant from the dropdown above to view securities data'
4. The API calls already include X-GarudaX-Tenant header from T1 — no changes needed to API calls

Also add tenant_id display in the DataGrid empty message: 'No instruments found for {tenantName}'

CX ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all must pass):
□ Loading state: existing loading states preserved
□ Empty state: tenant-specific empty message
□ Error state: existing error handling preserved
□ Responsive: tenant name in title wraps on mobile
□ Keyboard navigation: N/A (no new interactive elements)
□ Accessible: N/A
□ Toast/feedback: N/A
□ Form validation: N/A
□ No raw technical errors visible to user — handle null currentTenant gracefully
□ Consistent styling: tenant name uses existing heading styles

Run: cd src/admin-ui && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test -- --run

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T3.md

tasks.title: CX review: tenant-aware admin UI
tasks.description:
Review all tenant-aware UI changes against the CX checklist. Score 10 items. Fix gaps. Target >= 7/10.

Verify:
1. TenantProvider loads tenants without blocking UI
2. TopBar selector shows tenant name + status, is keyboard accessible
3. DashboardHome shows current tenant prominently
4. Securities pages show tenant name in title
5. API calls include X-GarudaX-Tenant header (check apiFetch)
6. localStorage persists tenant selection
7. No 'undefined' or 'null' when no tenant selected
8. Responsive: selector works on mobile
9. Toast on tenant switch
10. Consistent styling throughout

Run: cd src/admin-ui && npm test -- --run

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T4.md with CX score and fixes.
